result.summary: Kong exposes regional Konnect HTTP APIs, a regional Konnect MCP server, and three documented CLIs: kongctl, decK, and Inso CLI.
result.description: Kong provides API gateway and API platform products, including the hosted Konnect platform for managing gateway configuration, developer portals, analytics, and related API infrastructure. It also offers developer tools such as decK, kongctl, and Insomnia's Inso CLI.

konnect_pat.label: Konnect Personal Access Token (PAT)
konnect_pat.setup: In [Kong Konnect API auth](https://developer.konghq.com/konnect-api/) and [decK get started](https://developer.konghq.com/deck/get-started/), Kong says to create a personal access token from the Konnect PAT page. Sign in to [Konnect](https://cloud.konghq.com/), open the [PAT page](https://cloud.konghq.com/global/account/tokens), select **Generate Token**, and copy the token. Use it as a bearer token, for example in `Authorization: Bearer <token>` or via CLI flags/env vars documented by the tool.

konnect_device_login.label: Konnect device-flow login for kongctl
konnect_device_login.setup: For `kongctl`, run [`kongctl login`](https://developer.konghq.com/kongctl/authentication/). The CLI starts a device authorization flow, shows a one-time code, and asks you to open [device activation](https://cloud.konghq.com/device-activate) in your browser. After you authorize, `kongctl` stores and refreshes the access/refresh tokens locally in `~/.config/kongctl/` (or `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/kongctl/`).

variables.name: region
variables.resolveFrom: Konnect region; installation docs show `https://us.mcp.konghq.com/` and refer to regional server URLs

variables.name: region
variables.resolveFrom: Konnect region, e.g. `us` from docs examples like `https://us.api.konghq.com`
